Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 51 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. My Favorite 39C3 Talks 52 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 159 upvotes, 19 comments
  4. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 111 upvotes, 18 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 224 upvotes, 51 comments
  6. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 255 upvotes, 63 comments
  7. The Rust calling convention we deserve (2024) 55 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Show HN: A shell-native cd-compatible directory jumper using power-law frecency 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 202 upvotes, 59 comments
  10. NRC Issues First Commercial Reactor Construction Approval in 10 Years [pdf] 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. “It turns out” (2010) 230 upvotes, 74 comments
  12. The View from RSS 59 upvotes, 14 comments
  13. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 105 upvotes, 30 comments
  14. An interactive map of Flock Cams 482 upvotes, 186 comments
  15. Flip Distance of Convex Triangulations and Tree Rotation Is NP-Complete 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 479 upvotes, 229 comments
  17. Approximation Game 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 49 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 60 upvotes, 28 comments
  20. Extending single-minus amplitudes to gravitons 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Glaze by Raycast 187 upvotes, 114 comments
  22. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 241 upvotes, 169 comments
  23. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 60 upvotes, 39 comments
  24. Show HN: Vertex.js – A 1kloc SPA Framework 34 upvotes, 20 comments
  25. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 40 upvotes, 25 comments
  26. Does that use a lot of energy? 172 upvotes, 142 comments
  27. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 58 upvotes, 51 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1462 upvotes, 1814 comments
  29. Daemon (2006) 7 upvotes, 3 comments
